title: Rett syndrome - NHS
text:
f hand control, walking ability and communication skills, most will be dependent on 24-hour care throughout their lives.
Many people with Rett syndrome reach adulthood, and those who are less severely affected can live into old age. However, some people die at a fairly young age as a result of complications, such as heart rhythm abnormalities,  pneumonia and epilepsy .
Advice for carers 
Caring for a child with Rett syndrome is mentally and physically challenging. Most carers will need social and psychological support.
The social care and support guide provides lots of information and advice about how you can take time to look after yourself, including:
getting a break from caring benefits for carers Rett UK is a charity providing help and support for people living with Rett syndrome. Their helpline number is 01582 798911, and you can also contact them by email: support@rettuk.org
